figures.caption: Figure 3: Projected band structure and density of states at the HSE06 level theory. Color code: orange and blue for Pd and Te atoms, respectively.
Table 3: Calculated d-band center for adsorbed intermediates on strained and unstrained penta-PdTe2. Here * represents the penta-PdTe2 monolayer.

Figure 4: Band gap alignment of pristine penta-PdTe2 with respect to applied biaxial strain. The dotted red and blue lines represent the reduction and oxidation potentials at pH 0 and 7, respectively.

Table 2: calculated carrier mobility 𝜇𝑐 (𝑐𝑚 2𝑉−1𝑆−1) along x/y-axis, and calculated overpotential for HER (𝜂𝐻𝐸𝑅) and OER (𝜂𝑂𝐸𝑅) and their comparison with hexagonal PdTe2 and other pentagonal materials at pH = 0 and U = 0.
Figure 6: Calculated ∆G for the HER at 𝑈 = 0 (black), 𝑈 = 𝑈𝑒(red), and 𝑈 > 𝑅𝐷𝑆(blue) at (a) pH = 0 and (b) pH = 7, and for the OER at (c) pH = 0 and (d) pH = 7 under +3% biaxial strain.
Figure 1: Top and side views of the optimized structure of the pristine pentagonal PdTe2 monolayer.

Table 4: Calculated available photon energy (E), energy conversion efficiency of light absorption (𝜂𝑎𝑏𝑠), carrier utilization (𝜂𝑐𝑢), and theoretical STH (𝜂𝑆𝑇𝐻) of penta-PdTe2 monolayer with and without biaxial strain (Ɛ𝑥𝑦).
Figure 5: Calculated free energy change for HER at potential U = 0 (black line), U = Ue (red line) and U > RDS (blue line) at a) pH = 0, b) pH = 7, and OER at c) pH = 0, d) pH = 7.
Table 1: Calculated effective mass (𝑚∗), deformation 𝐸𝑑 (eV) and in plane elastic modulus C2D (N/m) along x and y-axis and their comparison with hexagonal-PdTe2 and other pentagonal materials.
Figure 2: Pentagonal PdTe2 monolayer: (a) The charge density difference (CDD) plot, and (b) planaraverage projection along the z-axis ρ(z). The iso-surface value is 0.005 e/Å3.
